people.summary: Marc Rico-Pastó is a postdoctoral researcher working at the interface of physics and biomedicine. His research focuses on single-molecule biophysics, feedback thermodynamics, and mechanobiology applied to cancer research. He studies the mechanical properties of lung cancer cells using force-spectroscopy techniques to understand tumor progression and metastasis. His previous work explored molecular folding, fluctuation theorems, and non-equilibrium thermodynamics through experimental and theoretical approaches.
people.name: Marc Rico-Pastó

people.summary: Alejandro Bernardo is a predoctoral researcher who is focused on understanding how the tumor microenvironment contributes to the metastatic progression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), with particular emphasis on fibroblast heterogeneity, stromal remodeling and collagen architecture. By combining molecular biology approaches and functional assays including signaling studies, he investigates how the tumor and the stroma interact to promote metastatic dissemination.
people.name: Alejandro Bernardo Suarez

people.summary: Victoria Batto is a graduate researcher working on translational cancer research focused on the tumor microenvironment and the immunosuppressive role of tumor-associated fibroblasts in lung cancer. She combines experience from the pharmaceutical industry with strong training in molecular and cellular biology techniques, including immunology, virology, and functional cell assays.
people.name: Victoria Batto
people.area: Tumor Microenvironment, Immunology, Lung Cancer Research

people.summary: Teia Vallès Pagès is a predoctoral researcher studying cancer biology and metastasis. Her work focuses on understanding the role of tumor-associated fibroblasts (TAFs) in HER2+ breast cancer metastasis, particularly during the circulation stage of the metastatic cascade. Using a biomimetic fluidic system developed in the lab, she investigates how shear stress affects circulating tumor cell survival and how TAFs may enhance it through mechanisms such as clustering and paracrine signaling. Her expertise includes cell culture, fluidic systems and organ-on-chip models, confocal microscopy, and molecular analysis techniques. Teia previously conducted research abroad, including her bachelor’s thesis in Lund, Sweden, and is also involved in science outreach initiatives such as “La Festa de la Ciència” and “Petites Mirades”, both from the University of Barcelona (UB).

education.degree: M.Sc. in Biomedicine (Basic and Translational Cancer Research)

people.name: Teia Vallès
people.area: Cancer biology, Breast Cancer Metastasis, Tumor microenvironment

people.summary: Senior pathologist specializing in thoracic pathology, with a strong association to the laboratory and an active role in translational research. My work focuses on the detailed histological characterization of thoracic tumors and the comprehensive assessment of immunohistochemical biomarkers, integrating diagnostic expertise with research-driven approaches. Over a decade of work at Hospital de Sant Pau collaborating closely with oncology research groups, contributing to projects that integrate diagnostic pathology with molecular and translational approaches. Particularly interested in tumour biology, biomarkers, and bridging clinical practice with research innovation.
people.name: Laura López Vilaró

people.summary: Medical image computing engineer specializing in quantitative medical imaging, AI-driven analysis, and research software development. His work focuses on building image processing pipelines for biomedical applications, including histopathology segmentation, registration and feature extraction. He combines experience in medical device R&D, regulatory environments, and interdisciplinary collaboration to translate computational methods into practical healthcare solutions.

education.degree: M.Sc. in Design and Management of Technological Projects

people.name: José Rodríguez

people.summary: Nanthilde Malandain is a postdoctoral researcher working on tissue nanomechanics, particularly in the context of lung cancer. Her current research is conducted through a collaboration involving ARTIDIS (Switzerland), with a focus on nanomechanical profiling for diagnostic and treatment optimization applications. Her previous work focused on the development of physiomimetic biomaterials and investigate how microenvironmental cues shape mesenchymal stromal cell behavior.

education.degree: M.Sc. in Molecular and Cellular Biology

people.name: Nanthilde Malandain
